title: Ford brings back veteran engineers after AI quality systems fall short
friendlyTitle: Ford learned AI still needs experienced engineers
summary: Ford has rehired about 350 veteran engineers after automated quality systems and AI did not deliver the quality gains executives expected, according to TechCrunch’s summary of Bloomberg reporting.
friendlySummary: Ford brought back hundreds of veteran engineers after AI-based quality systems did not work well enough on their own.
whyItMatters: The story is a grounded reminder that domain expertise remains essential in high-stakes physical products. AI adoption works best when experienced people shape, test, and correct the systems rather than assuming automation alone will carry quality.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI can help, but it should not replace deep human know-how in safety-critical work. Machines still need the grey-beard squad.
